message:
fix(session,pkg): extract finish_worker_result helpers to join threads before checking cancellation

Refactor all spawn-then-recv patterns in session.rs and
package_manager.rs to call thread::JoinHandle::join() before treating
a closed oneshot channel as cancellation. Previously, if the background
thread panicked, the panic payload would be dropped when the JoinHandle
was not joined, and the caller would see a misleading "task cancelled"
error instead of propagating the panic.

session.rs:
- Add finish_worker_result<T,E>() generic helper: joins the thread
  first (re-unwinding any panic), then maps a closed channel to a
  session error with the provided message
- Add finish_jsonl_worker_result<E>() convenience wrapper for the
  common Result<()> case
- Apply to 4 call sites: open_from_v2_store (async), open_jsonl (async),
  save full-rewrite path, save append path, and scan_sessions_on_disk
- Add 3 tests: panic propagates before cancellation, non-panic
  cancellation maps to session error, success payload passes through

package_manager.rs:
- Add parallel finish_package_task<T,E>() helper with Error::tool()
  error constructor
- Apply to 7 call sites: install, remove, update_source,
  installed_path, resolve_extensions, add_package_source,
  remove_package_source

The pattern is identical in both files: capture the JoinHandle (was
previously discarded by the bare thread::spawn), pass it with the
recv result to the finish helper, which joins first and only then
checks the channel result.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
